title: Ligra Graph Processing Framework Evolution
summary: A 2-paper evolution chain for shared-memory graph processing. Paper 1 (Ligra, PPoPP 2013) introduces a lightweight parallel graph processing framework based on edgeMap/vertexMap abstractions with sparse/dense mode switching. Paper 2 (Ligra+, IEEE TOPC 2015) extends it with compressed graph representations using variable-length byte encoding and run-length encoding, reducing memory footprint while preserving the same algorithmic interface. Both papers evolve the same C++ codebase in place.
instructionPreview: **Overview**: This task involves implementing the Ligra graph processing framework evolution, following the published Ligra / Ligra+ research sequence. Ligra (PPoPP 2013) introduced a lightweight shared-memory parallel graph processing framework based on edgeMap/vertexMap abstractions. Ligra+ (IEEE TOPC 2015) extended it with compressed graph representations that reduce memory footprint while preserving the same algorithmic interface. Both papers share the same C++ codebase.
instruction:
**Overview**: This task involves implementing the Ligra graph processing framework evolution,
following the published Ligra / Ligra+ research sequence. Ligra (PPoPP 2013) introduced a lightweight shared-memory
parallel graph processing framework based on edgeMap/vertexMap abstractions. Ligra+ (IEEE TOPC 2015)
extended it with compressed graph representations that reduce memory footprint while preserving
the same algorithmic interface. Both papers share the same C++ codebase.

**Directory layout**:

- `/workspace/` -- base framework code is already present here. All papers build on top
  of these files in place. Do NOT create per-paper subdirectories.
- `/workspace/ligra/` -- framework header files (parallel.h, utils.h, graph.h, vertex.h, etc.)
- `/workspace/apps/` -- application directory with Makefile (applications must be implemented here)
- `/workspace/inputs/` -- test graphs (rMatGraph_J_5_100, rMatGraph_WJ_5_100)
- `/workspace/run.sh` -- placeholder experiment runner that must be replaced with the final implementation

**Build system**: The apps/Makefile uses `make` with g++. Applications are compiled as
`g++ -std=c++14 -O3 -DBYTERLE -o <app> <app>.C`. Each .C file in apps/ is symlinked to
header files from ../ligra/. Run `make` from the apps/ directory to build all targets.

**DAG units**: The task now uses paper-level implementation units with matching
requirement files under `requirements/`: `ligra` and `ligra_plus`.

**Implementation workflow** (MUST follow this order):

1. Implement ALL mechanisms of Paper 1 (Ligra) inside `/workspace/`
2. Implement ALL of Paper 2 (Ligra+) NEW mechanisms on top, still inside `/workspace/`

**IMPORTANT**: The workspace must contain a COMPLETE, working implementation after each paper.
Implement every mechanism described in each paper in full. Do not skip any algorithm,
data structure, or component mentioned in the paper.

---

## Paper 1: ligra -- Ligra: A Lightweight Graph Processing Framework for Shared Memory

**Working directory**: `/workspace/`

**Mechanisms to implement** (implement ALL of the following):

- **vertexSubset data structure**: A set of active vertices represented in two modes:
  sparse (a list of vertex IDs) and dense (a boolean array of length n). The data structure
  must support converting between representations via toSparse() and toDense(), and provide
  size queries (numRows, numNonzeros, isEmpty). Use a threshold (default: n/20) to decide
  when to switch between sparse and dense processing.

- **edgeMap function**: The core traversal primitive. Given a graph G, a vertexSubset U of
  active vertices, and user-defined functions (update, updateAtomic, cond), iterate over all
  edges (u,v) where u is in U. Must support two modes:
  - Sparse mode: iterate over out-neighbors of each vertex in U. Used when the frontier is small.
  - Dense mode: iterate over all vertices v and check if any in-neighbor is in U. Used when
    frontier is large (m + outDegrees > threshold).
  edgeMap should dispatch between sparse and dense based on frontier size vs. the threshold.
  Sparse mode must support both filtered and no-filter variants. Dense mode must support
  both backward (iterate in-neighbors) and forward (iterate out-neighbors) variants.
  All modes must handle the output flag (whether to produce a new vertexSubset) and the
  remove_duplicates flag (dedup output vertices).

- **vertexMap function**: Apply a user function f to every vertex in a vertexSubset.
  Must handle both sparse and dense representations, and both data and no-data variants.

- **vertexFilter function**: Return a new vertexSubset containing only vertices that satisfy
  a predicate. Must support both dense-based filtering (vertexFilter) and sparse-based
  filtering (vertexFilter2).

- **BFS application**: Breadth-first search from a source vertex. Use edgeMap with an update
  function that sets parent[d] = s when d is unvisited (parent[d] == UINT_E_MAX), and a cond
  function that checks if d is unvisited. Loop until the frontier is empty. Count and report
  the number of BFS levels (rounds) and the number of visited vertices.

- **Triangle counting application**: Count the exact number of triangles in a symmetric graph.
  For each vertex u, for each neighbor v > u, count the intersection of N(u) and N(v) where
  the third vertex w > v. Use the countOutNgh primitive for efficient counting.
  Report the total triangle count.

- **PageRank application**: Iterative PageRank with damping factor 0.85. Initialize all
  vertices with rank 1/n. In each iteration: (a) spread rank along edges using edgeMap
  (each vertex distributes its rank equally to all out-neighbors), (b) apply the damping
  formula: p_next[i] = damping * p_next[i] + (1-damping)/n. Converge when L1-norm of
  the difference is below epsilon=0.0000001, or after maxiters iterations. Report the sum
  of all final PageRank values.

- **Other applications**: Also implement BellmanFord (single-source shortest paths),
  Components (connected components via label propagation), Components-Shortcut (with
  pointer jumping), KCore (k-core decomposition), MIS (maximal independent set),
  BC (betweenness centrality), Radii (graph radii estimation), and PageRankDelta.
  These share the edgeMap/vertexMap interface.

**Experiments to run** (run from `/workspace/`):
- `bfs_level_count`: Run BFS from vertex 0 on the symmetric graph rMatGraph_J_5_100.
  Build and run: `cd apps && make BFS && ./BFS -r 0 -s -rounds 1 ../inputs/rMatGraph_J_5_100`.
  Parse "BFS levels: <N>" from stdout. Report N as an integer.
- `triangle_count`: Count triangles on the symmetric graph rMatGraph_J_5_100.
  Build and run: `cd apps && make Triangle && ./Triangle -s -rounds 1 ../inputs/rMatGraph_J_5_100`.
  Parse "triangle count = <N>" from stdout. Report N as an integer.

---

## Paper 2: ligra_plus -- Smaller and Faster: Parallel Processing of Compressed Graphs with Ligra+

**Working directory**: `/workspace/` (same directory, building on Paper 1's implementation)

**Mechanisms to implement** (implement ALL of the following):

- **Byte encoding (variable-length delta encoding)**: Encode sorted neighbor lists using
  delta encoding with variable-length byte representation. For a sorted neighbor list
  [v0, v1, v2, ...], store [v0, v1-v0, v2-v1, ...] where each delta is encoded in 1-5 bytes
  using a continuation-bit scheme: low 7 bits = data, bit 7 = continuation flag (1 = more
  bytes follow). Must support both sequential and parallel decoding.

- **Byte-RLE encoding**: Extend byte encoding with run-length encoding. When the same delta
  value repeats k times consecutively, store (delta, count) instead of k separate copies.
  This benefits graphs with regular degree distributions.

- **compressedVertex interface**: A replacement for the standard vertex struct that provides
  the same interface (getOutNeighbors, getOutDegree, decodeOutNgh, decodeInNghBreakEarly,
  decodeOutNghSparse, decodeOutNghSparseSeq, countOutNgh, packOutNgh, copyOutNgh) but
  reads from compressed byte streams instead of raw arrays. Must work with both
  compressedSymmetricVertex and compressedAsymmetricVertex types.

- **Encoder tool**: A standalone program that reads an uncompressed Ligra graph file and
  writes a compressed version. The compressed file format has: (1) a header with vertex count,
  edge count, and total compressed space, (2) per-vertex byte offsets, (3) per-vertex degrees,
  (4) compressed neighbor data. Must handle both symmetric and asymmetric graphs.

- **Decoder tool**: A standalone program that reads a compressed graph and writes an
  uncompressed version (inverse of the encoder).

- **Integration with existing framework**: The existing edgeMap, vertexMap, and parallel_main
  functions must work with compressed graph types. When the -c flag is passed, parallel_main
  should read a compressed graph and pass it to Compute. All existing applications (BFS,
  PageRank, Triangle, etc.) must work unchanged on compressed graphs.

- **Update the Makefile**: Add encoder and decoder to the build targets, and add the
  compression header files (byte.h, byteRLE.h, byte-pd.h, byteRLE-pd.h, nibble.h,
  nibble-pd.h, encoder.C, decoder.C) to the COMMON dependencies list.

**Experiments to run** (run from `/workspace/`):
- `bfs_compressed_matches_uncompressed`: Compress rMatGraph_J_5_100 using the encoder,
  then run BFS from vertex 0 on the compressed graph. Verify visited count matches
  uncompressed BFS.
  Steps: `cd apps && make encoder BFS && ./encoder ../inputs/rMatGraph_J_5_100 ../inputs/rMatGraph_J_5_100.compressed && ./BFS -r 0 -c -s -rounds 1 ../inputs/rMatGraph_J_5_100.compressed`.
  Parse "BFS visited: <N>" from stdout. Report N as an integer.
- `pagerank_sum_compressed`: Run PageRank with 10 iterations on the compressed graph.
  Steps: `cd apps && make PageRank && ./PageRank -maxiters 10 -c -s -rounds 1 ../inputs/rMatGraph_J_5_100.compressed`.
  Parse "PageRank sum: <F>" from stdout. Report F as a float.

---

## run.sh Specification

After implementing all papers, write `/workspace/run.sh` that:
1. Runs ALL experiments for ALL papers
2. Writes results to `/workspace/results.json` in this exact format:
   ```json
   {
     "ligra": {
       "bfs_level_count": <integer>,
       "triangle_count": <integer>
     },
     "ligra_plus": {
       "bfs_compressed_matches_uncompressed": <integer>,
       "pagerank_sum_compressed": <float>
     }
   }
   ```
3. All experiments run from `cd /workspace/`
4. Total experiment count in results.json must equal 4
5. All values must be numeric (int or float)

moduleDag.description: A 2-paper evolution chain for shared-memory graph processing. Paper 1 (Ligra, PPoPP 2013) introduces a lightweight parallel graph processing framework based on edgeMap/vertexMap abstractions with sparse/dense mode switching. Paper 2 (Ligra+, IEEE TOPC 2015) extends it with compressed graph representations using variable-length byte encoding and run-length encoding, reducing memory footprint while preserving the same algorithmic interface. Both papers evolve the same C++ codebase in place.

nodes.label: Framework Core (edgeMap/vertexMap)
nodes.description: Implements the core graph traversal primitives: edgeMapDense (backward dense traversal over in-neighbors), edgeMapDenseForward (forward dense traversal over out-neighbors), edgeMapSparse (sparse frontier iteration), edgeMapSparse_no_filter (block-parallel sparse without filtering), edgeMapData (main dispatcher selecting sparse vs dense based on frontier size threshold), vertexMap (applies function to active vertices), vertexFilter/vertexFilter2 (subset filtering), packEdges, and edgeMapFilter. This is the central dispatch layer of the Ligra framework.

nodes.label: Core Ligra Applications
nodes.description: Graph algorithm source files using the edgeMap/vertexMap interface: BFS (breadth-first search), Triangle (exact triangle counting), PageRank (iterative with damping), BC (betweenness centrality), BellmanFord (single-source shortest paths), Components (connected components via label propagation), Components-Shortcut (with pointer jumping), KCore (k-core decomposition), MIS (maximal independent set), Radii (graph radii estimation), PageRankDelta, BFSCC, BFS-Bitvector, and CF (collaborative filtering).

nodes.label: Compression Codecs
nodes.description: Variable-length delta encoding schemes for compressed neighbor lists. Includes byte encoding (byte.h, byte-pd.h) using 7-bit continuation scheme, byte-RLE encoding (byteRLE.h, byteRLE-pd.h) extending byte encoding with run-length encoding for repeated deltas, and nibble encoding (nibble.h, nibble-pd.h) using 4-bit units. Each codec has both sequential and parallel decoding variants (-pd suffix). All codecs encode sorted neighbor lists as [first_edge, delta1, delta2, ...].

nodes.label: Compressed Vertex Interface
nodes.description: Full implementation of compressedSymmetricVertex and compressedAsymmetricVertex structs that provide the same interface as standard vertices (decodeOutNgh, decodeInNghBreakEarly, decodeOutNghSparse, decodeOutNghSparseSeq, countOutNgh, packOutNgh, copyOutNgh) but read from compressed byte streams. Dispatches to the appropriate codec (byte/byteRLE/nibble) via preprocessor flags. Includes helper functors for dense, denseForward, sparse, and sparseWgh traversal patterns.

nodes.label: Graph Encoder/Decoder Tools
nodes.description: Standalone programs for graph compression and decompression. The encoder reads an uncompressed Ligra adjacency graph file, computes delta-encoded compressed neighbor lists using all three codecs (byte, byteRLE, nibble), selects the smallest representation per vertex, and writes a compressed graph file with header (vertex count, edge count, compressed size), per-vertex offsets, degrees, and compressed data. The decoder inverts this process. Both handle symmetric and asymmetric graphs. Includes the top-level apps/Makefile integration for encoder/decoder targets and the final /workspace/run.sh experiment runner.

nodes.label: Extended Algorithm Applications
nodes.description: Additional graph algorithms organized in subdirectories: bucketing/ (DeltaStepping SSSP, KCore with bucket-based peeling, SetCover), eccentricity/ (CLRSTV, FM-Ecc, LogLog-Ecc, RV, TK, kBFS variants for graph eccentricity/diameter estimation), and localAlg/ (ACL local clustering variants, HeatKernel diffusion, Nibble graph partitioning). Each subdirectory has its own Makefile and helper headers.

nodes.label: Hypergraph Framework Extension
nodes.description: Extends the Ligra framework to support hypergraph processing. Includes hygra.h (hypergraph-aware edgeMap/vertexMap), hypergraphIO.h (I/O routines for reading/writing hypergraph files in compressed and uncompressed formats), hypergraphEncoder.C and hypergraphDecoder.C (compression tools for hypergraph files). Hypergraph vertices have both vertex-neighbors and hyperedge-neighbors.

nodes.label: Hypergraph Applications
nodes.description: Graph algorithms adapted for hypergraph processing: HyperBFS, HyperBC (betweenness centrality), HyperBPath (bounded path), HyperCC (connected components), HyperKCore and HyperKCore-Efficient (k-core decomposition), HyperMIS (maximal independent set), HyperPageRank, HyperSSSP (shortest paths), HyperReader, and WeightedHyperReader. Built using the hypergraph framework (hygra.h).

edges.label: compressed vertex dispatches to byte/byteRLE/nibble codec
encoder/decoder uses all codecs via compressed vertex types
encoder uses graph IO and parallel primitives
hygra.h extends ligra.h edgeMap for hypergraphs
